entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content: Did you release the process order after creation ?

entry.title: Re: User authorization is not working in BEX Query
entry.content:
Hi 

Is the company code characteristic the relevant authorization? if it yes,then you can specific the company code.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: With the new Full People Profile have a section like...
entry.content: Userid is not available in the profile, and regarding username, login name instead of username is displaying next to employee name. It requires permission: User Account OData entity. Could you please grant the permission and then check if it is visible?

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content: Ajit - When I create the Process Order via COR1 tcode, the order is already in a REL CRCR status. I can't find another tcode to use for the release. As for the PSA in EWM, I have already maintained this as well as the business functions. I have followed the Advanced Production Integration config guide from SAP.

entry.title: Re: sap gui installation issue
entry.content:
Hi

Support for SAPGUI 7.60 ended in 2022. You can try the latest version, SAPGUI 800, along with the most recent patch.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: additional Application Server
entry.content:
Hi Sim,

Have you initiated the additional application instance using the SAP SIDADM user ID? Please ensure you are not using the root user ID.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: SUSG integration with ATC
entry.content:
Hi @KumarAakash ,

To echo on @manuel_horn3 's reply, in the blog you can find [all custom code analysis options at one glance]:
1. Remote ATC with SAP GUI(required system SAP_BASIS 752)
2. Remote ATC with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration(required system SAP S/4HANA >=1809)
3.Remote ATC with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration(required system SAP BTP ABAP Environment). 

So if you don't have S/4HANA system or SAP BTP, but have ECC system on SAP_BASIS 752 or 753, you can consider using Option 1. Remote ATC with SAP GUI. This is also mentioned in Readiness Checking note 2913617:
"The ABAP test cockpit is an alternative custom code analysis tool supported by SAP Readiness Check. The ABAP test cockpit will perform a more in-depth analysis in comparison to the Custom Code Analyzer. However, it must be highlighted that there are additional steps required to enable this analysis. The process for establishing an ABAP test cockpit system for performing this analysis is documented here.

SAP Note 2781766 must be implemented on the system where the ABAP test cockpit is installed and enabled. If that system is >= SAP_BASIS 7.53 SP3 or >= SAP_BASIS 7.52 SP5, SAP Note 2781766 is not required, as the functionality is already available in those releases.

To create the ZIP archive in the ABAP test cockpit for upload to SAP Readiness Check, select the context menu option Export to -> File for SAP Readiness Check in the ABAP test cockpit result browser.

Similar to the Customer Code Analyzer, the ABAP test cockpit analysis is conducted outside of the program RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA and is later uploaded to the analysis created on the landing page of SAP Readiness Check."

Best Regards,
David

entry.title: Hide Transport Change document type from Solution documentation dropdown
entry.content:
We are using Change control for Solution documentation, and we are using a separate transaction type(ZMAD – DIGITS*) to make the changes in SolDoc apart from the change document that will be used for transports(ZMMJ- DIGITS*).

We would like to hide the change document types that are used for transports (ZMMJ) from the change document dropdown in SolDoc but would like to refer documentation in them (manual assignment through single selection and multiple selection buttons). Please suggest if this is possible.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Hide Transport Change document type from Solution doc...
entry.content: The function you require is not included in the SAP standard system for Solution Manager 7.2.

entry.title: How to get details of all authorization checks in Programs and subprograms included in it?
entry.content:
How to get details of all authorization checks in Programs and subprograms included in it?

Earlier we used to get this data via RSABAPSC but now it is obsolete in newer releases. Any help appreciated.

Could you try it again with another non-GR customization to see if it is changeable? Or you can raise a case to cloud operation team with component XX-S4C-OPR-SRV for further clarification.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Public Cloud Group reporting why does mast...
entry.content: You only need to specify the global parameter when a new user logs in for the first time or if a change is required. In all other cases, it doesn’t need to be re-entered.

entry.title: Re: My Inbox Showing Blank

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950

entry.title: Re: How can we change Labels for a separate Site, without...
entry.content:
Hello @Nehar ,

Unfortunately, Currently it is not possible to update labels for a particular site.

Updating the label would affect home page for both users. Internal and External.

You may raise an enhancement request around this if you think this feature might be helpful and should be a part of SuccessFactors Learning.

 If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Swapnil

entry.title: Re: Sample File Link on Detail Screen - Agent Performance Management
entry.content: Not specific to a broker/customer. I want the sample file be added to the form so that it's visible in every record. I did not see any option to add a link in customised forms so was looking for alternatives.

entry.title: Screen persona profiler or similar tool for Zebra RF devices
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We build and use sap screen persona flavors on Zebra RF devices, flavors are built for transaction /SCWM/RFUI. For many screen users are complaining that screen loads very slowly and took more time. Screen for which user is complaining does have any script, I tried to use same flavors on laptop, and it do some slowness but when see same flavors on RF device it really takes more time. So is there any tool or way available which could help locating the object which is causing this delay?

I am searching for a tool which could help me analyzing the performance of flavors when we run it on Zebra mobile devices. I tried activating "Screen persona profiler", its visible on laptop browser but I do not see this option when flavor is launched on mobile screen. Is there a way to activate it for mobile devices? 

If not is there any other tool or available in market which could integrate with SAP without putting any extra load and help analyzing the performance?

Apricate your replies, thoughts and ideas. 

Thanks in advance. 

Regards

Ritz

entry.title: Re: Cannot log into AI Launchpad

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content: That is correct. PMR should create without staging component.

entry.title: Re: Sales Return Copy Data
entry.content: From April 1, divisions 20 and 21 will be closed. Since sales were invoiced under these divisions, SAP copies all header data when creating a return order through billing document reference. new requirement is, from April 1 onward, any return order for these divisions should be processed under the new division 22.

else do we have any SAP accounts to test the sample einvoicing? 

Thank you

entry.title: Cost planning in Cj40 is not availble in Cj30 to distribute
entry.content: User has plan the cost in Cj40 for WBS element ST-0138-L , however in Cj30 when trying to distribute the cost that WBS element is greyed out and cost is not available to distribute . Please help ti understand why this is happening.

entry.title: 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule - (Step #2) How Joule Works
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series 8 Baby Steps to Learn Joule. In the previous blog #1 – Introduction to Joule, we learnt what Joule is and how customers can use it. 

In this post, we will learn how Joule works.

Let's begin!

 

 

Step 1 – User Enters the Prompt

Users start with their queries in the natively integrated Joule client embedded in SAP cloud solutions, such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors etc.

 

Step 2 – The request is processed and orchestrated by Joule

1. First, Joule analyses if the prompt is relevant for Joule’s Scenario Catalog.

What is Joule's Scenario Catalog?

Joule's Scenario Catalog is a comprehensive repository that contains metadata of all available scenarios, functions, and skills across SAP cloud applications. When a user interacts with Joule, their input is first analysed against this catalog to determine if it aligns with any predefined scenarios or functionalities. This process ensures that Joule can provide accurate and contextually relevant responses based on the capabilities defined within the SAP ecosystem.

2. Further, Joule conducts an informational filtering based on the Knowledge Catalog. This process is based on RAG and lets the LLM generate an answer which is contextually relevant and precise. 

Knowledge Catalog is a repository that contains SAP’s proprietary knowledge and customer owned knowledge via Document Grounding.

3. Joule is aware of the user's context and chat history.

This includes information about which SAP cloud application the respective user is using and which additional SAP application the customer has licensed and activated with Joule.

Also, Joule is aware of the user's role(s) and permissions. This means a user cannot access information or adjust business processes they couldn't do directly in the SAP cloud application without using Joule.

 

Step 3 – Joule sends an enhanced query to LLM

Joule takes all of this information and provides a much richer query to the LLM.

These LLMs are also available directly via SAP’s Generative AI Hub, which is part of the SAP BTP.

 

Which LLM is used by Joule?

Joule follows SAP‘s general multi-partner strategy for generative AI.  Joule may employ a variety of foundational models to deliver the best performance for each use case. This ensures that customers get benefitted from the most relevant LLM.

Joule leverages only those LLMs which are available for SAP customer and partners within Generative AI Hub in AI Foundation in SAP BTP.

SAP has under strict contractual agreements with LLM providers which prohibits them from training any of their AI-models on any SAP’s customer data.

 

Step 4 – LLM’s response back to Joule

Next, the LLM provides a grounded response back to the Joule service.

This is:

  • Either the response based on its training data and knowledge which could be sent directly to the user
  • or there is a need to leverage Joule functions which includes the Joule scenarios of SAP Cloud Applications.

In simple words, if the user’s query requires action within an SAP system or information from SAP system, the LLM identifies the need to call Joule functions, which connect to SAP Cloud Applications (e.g., S/4HANA, SuccessFactors).

 

Step 5 – Joule Calls the SAP Backend System

If required, Joule leverages Joule functions to call an SAP backend system.

1. Joule sends the request to the relevant SAP backend system.

For this, the respective information is retrieved via Odata calls OR the LLM for dialog management calls another LLM which was used by an SAP Cloud Application to develop a specific scenario (e.g. interview question generation).

2. Joule gets the required information, processes it.

3. The response from SAP is also filtered and validated to ensure it meets enterprise security, data privacy, and responsible AI guidelines. This step ensures that only secure, accurate, and appropriate responses are sent back to the user.

 

Step 6 - Final Response to User

The user receives a response, knowing that the conversation and output has been processed all with enterprise security, data privacy, and responsible AI considered.

 

This is in nutshell - How Joule works!

 

𝐃𝐫𝐨𝐩 𝐚 "𝐘𝐞𝐬" 𝐢𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬 𝐢𝐟 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐜𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐥𝐲 👇

 

Stay tuned for Blog #3 - Joule Architecture.  Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset:Posting Acquisitions without Order
entry.content: Dear Customer, Please Activate Direct Posting to G/L & Material Accounts & Assets (ID in the ‘Configure Your Solution’ configuration environment: 106330). Please refer to the KBA 3369087 - Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (MIRO) app - missing Asset tab (https://me.sap.com/notes/3369087) for more details. Best Regards, Evelyn

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content:
Hi @Dhood ,

PMR is designed as per below process. and for GR, you can create EGR process if you want.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/1b7fec53d3eb5514e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENDED_WAREHOUSE_MANAGEMENT/3d97bec9bf1649099384bb8167df3cf2/c399eb535cbb5d1ee10000000a441470.html

Production Material Request

Definition

The production material request is a document containing all the warehouse-relevant data for a manufacturing order (a production order or process order).

Use

You use the production material request for planning and monitoring the supply of products to production, and for the consumption of products by production, using advanced production integration.

The system creates a production material request when SAP ERP sends a message containing the required information from production. SAP ERP sends this message when the manufacturing order is released, and WM material staging is triggered, either automatically or in the Production Order Change transaction, either by choosing Function  WM Material Staging  Execute, or by clicking the WM Material Staging button.

If the manufacturing order is changed in SAP ERP, the system updates the production material request in SAP EWM for changes that are relevant for SAP EWM, such as a changed quantity for staging.

To view production material requests, on the SAP Easy Access screen for Extended Warehouse Management, choose Work Scheduling  Staging for Production  Maintain Production Material Request.

 
Please let us know if further questions.
 
Kind Regards,
Ajit

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud compatibility with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Is there any feature support matrix for SAP Analytics Cloud functionality for Datasphere (direct consume for analytics model)?

We have done few testing and found that nice feature such Compass, Predictive Scenario, In chart time series forecast and smart insight is not working.

Thanks for all help.

entry.title: Re: How to attach the uploaded files of sap BPA to email
entry.content: You can't upload files directly to the email notification in the process flow. for such kind of thing you will need to create separate automation.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story date Issue
entry.content: would you please rephrase your question a bit? It's difficult to understand.

entry.title: Fiori-Style Analytics Dashboard: 'Invalid Username/Password' Error for Non-Manager Users
entry.content:
After logging into SAP Business One, our system automatically loads the Fiori-style Analytics dashboard (Pervasive Analytics). While the Manager user has no issues accessing the dashboard, other users consistently receive an "Invalid Username/Password" error when the dashboard loads.

Details:

  • SAP Business One Version: Sap Business One 10 FP 2502
  • Database: SAP HANA (SLD connection user is SYSTEM)  - version Hana 2.00.059
  • Issue: After successful login to SAP Business One, the Fiori-style dashboard attempts to load and fails for non-manager users with the error "Invalid Username/Password".
  • Manager User: Works perfectly without any errors.
  • Other Users: Despite having the required permissions for analytics, they are facing authentication issues only with the dashboard.

What I've Checked/Done:

  • User Permissions: Verified that all affected users have the necessary authorizations for Analytics and Pervasive Analytics.
  • SLD & Database Connection: Confirmed that the SLD database connection user is SYSTEM, which has all required privileges.

My Questions:

  1. What could cause the "Invalid Username/Password" error for non-manager users when accessing the Fiori-style dashboard, given that the Manager user works fine??
  2. Are there any known configuration settings or workarounds for ensuring that all users benefit from the SYSTEM user's privileges (like PAL_ROLE and AFL execution rights) when accessing Analytics?

entry.title: Re: DMR in Inspection lot vs Inspection Plan
entry.content: @Vignesh V May I ask where you captured the 1st screenshot on the DMR on characteristic level, please?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud compatibility with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi ,

Yes, there is a feature support matrix that outlines the functionalities available when connecting SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) directly to SAP Datasphere for analytics models. Establishing a live connection between SAC and SAP Datasphere allows you to leverage real-time data without replication. However, it's important to be aware of certain limitations and restrictions associated with this setup.

Analytics Limitations:

  • Map Visualizations: Only bubble, heat, choropleth, and flow maps are supported.
  • Data Blending: Merging data is restricted to datasets within the same SAP Datasphere space. Cross-space or cross-tenant data blending is not supported. Additionally, blending SAP Datasphere data with other live connections, such as SAP BW, is not possible.​
  • Advanced Features: Features like scheduling publications, R visualizations, and comment widgets are not supported. Linked dimensions are limited to models within the same space; cross-space linking is unavailable.

Also refer this note : SAP Note 2832606 – Unsupported Features with SAP Datasphere

Hope this helps. Thank you

regards

Giri

entry.title: Re: Work Center Auto Update in PM Order Confirmation
entry.content: Hi @Vaibav-Mishra, apologies for the delayed response, and thank you for your thoughtful input on my post—it’s much appreciated! That’s exactly what I’m aiming to achieve. The employee will carry out the work, and their ID will be populated accordingly. The confirmation will align with the work center linked to this employee, which could differ from the originally planned one. Additionally, no individual is assigned to more than one work center.

entry.title: Re: DVM Cloud App values vs DBACOCKPIT (HANA DB Memory Us...
entry.content:
Hi @jan_kotrc 

The used HANA Memory Usage(GB) = DRAM + Persistent Memory

For more information regarding Persistent Memory, please refer to the following note:
2700084 - FAQ: SAP HANA Persistent Memory

Best Regards,
David

entry.title: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud to SAP FSM
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm implementing the integration package - Integration of Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Field Service Management.

I have some failed messages of the integration flow - Replicate Service Order from SAP S4HANA Cloud to SAP Field Service Management because of the data error in SAP FSM. Now the data in FSM has been corrected.

How can I resend these failed messages from S4 to FSM?

Regards,

Minh

entry.title: Re: BP Roles for InHouse Banking

entry.title: Re: Query: Scope item BD6- Basic Credit Management

entry.title: Re: AS ABAP 752 SP04, developer edition: NOW AVAILABLE
entry.content:
Hi, 

Do anyone successfully installed the new license?? 

I tried but is not working for me so It would be helpful to confirm that new license works for You, 

Kind Regards

Roberto

entry.title: Re: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud...
entry.content:
Dear Minh,

Could you please check the Last Section mentioned on Monitoring Using Application Integration Framework in SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

Reprocessing the Replication

If there are any errors, you can reprocess the replication by selecting the log message and clicking the restart button. It is important to note that you cannot change the payload.

Also, kindly check Monitoring Using FSM Connector | SAP Help Portal 

Hope this helps!

Kind Regards,

Shubham

entry.title: Re: How to create an IM PI document for a SLOC which is t...
entry.content:
Create a Physical Inventory Count:
Go to MI09 (Create Inventory Count without Reference).
Enter the Plant and Storage Location (SLOC).
Add the material number and batch (if applicable).
Enter the count date and count class as needed.
Perform the Inventory Count:
Record the actual stock count in the system.
Post the Inventory Difference:
Use MI10 to post the inventory differences. This will update the stock levels accordingly.

entry.title: Re: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud...
entry.content: Hi Shubham, the link you sent me is for S/4HANA on premise. I'm using the public cloud version.

entry.title: Why the second ZP document is being created with full invoice amount (but says as bank charges)？
entry.content:
The first ZP document clears the vendor open line item. The debit in the second ZP (bank charges) clears the credit against the first ZP document. However, there is still a credit in the clearing account as an open item.

Could someone please explain the reason for the second ZP document, which includes both a debit and credit for the full amount of the invoice?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud...

Apologies.
Could you please check this section - Monitoring | SAP Help Portal

Many Thanks

Shubham

entry.title: Re: Table T169v
entry.content: Thanks for your help. Your way works.

entry.title: Re: How to resend failed messages in S/4HANA Public Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi Shubham,

I've checked the S4 cloud link you provided but it doesn't say anything about reprocess service order replication from S4 to FSM.

I can see it only mentioned "In the integration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Field Service Management, SAP Application Interface Framework is used to monitor the replication of master data and service contracts". Nothing about service orders.

Regards,

Minh

entry.title: Re: BP Roles for InHouse Banking
entry.content:
Hello,

It is not possible to define new Business Partner roles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Please carefully review the KBA below:

3268246 - Unable to create new Business Partner roles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Many thanks,

Liam

entry.title: Re: Offboarding 2.0

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE CR 827002 release info
entry.content: Hi, This CR appears to be related to LOB data corruption on standby ASE in HADR env. The fix is mainly on repserver side, and I can see one-off is provided based on 16.0.03.11 as well as 16.0.04.04. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Generate PDF forms in S/4 HANA Public Cloud (Developer extensibility) using BTP Adobe Forms serv
entry.content:
Hi @Bharath_Ramireddy 

Thank you for sharing your experience. I'm currently working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and I’m interested in how you handled spool (SP01) behavior in that context.

  • Did you manage the spool output via the Print Queue app in the public cloud?

  • How did you visualize the generated PDF from the spool, especially when the format is not directly printable (like for Zebra printers)?

  • Do you use an external output management system, or did you manage this with standard SAP cloud tools?

I would really appreciate any insight you can provide.

Thanks in advance!

It is not possible to re-trigger messages from monitor. 

Based on the failure, you can correct the errors in Service Order and then press Save.

It should get replicated

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Limit a YTD calculation in a column chart to a certai...
entry.content: I suppose if you only choose month from Jan to August in widget filter, you should still both current year YTD and also PY YTD for comparison. My example with 3 months selected.

entry.title: Pricing field Region (regio) is determining based on ship to party instead of sold to party
entry.content:
HI Team,

We have a standard price ZPR0 with access sequence Sales org/Material/Region . Right now, this condition type is determined correctly if the sold to party and ship to party are from the same region. But if, the sold to party is from different region say for example AN and if ship to party is TS, then the condition record is getting determined in the sales order based on the ship to party region and not from the sold to party. Is there any way in standard SAP to have the determination happen for sold to party region instead of ship to party region? Also if we remove a condition record for the ship to party region, then the system is not taking the condition record from sold to party region! Please let me know how to make the condition record preference for the sold to party region.

entry.title: Business process throwing could not translate value expression 'session.catalogversions'.
entry.content:
I have a custom business process action where I am trying to get associated catalog version from an order but getting exception. This is my code- 

((CMSSiteModel)cart.getSite()).getDefaultCatalog() - here I am getting catalog model
((CMSSiteModel)cart.getSite()).getDefaultCatalog().getCatalogVersions()- here my flexible search is throwing exception

Below is the exception

de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearchException: could not translate value expression 'session.catalogversions'
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.SessionParamTranslator.translatePathValueKeys(SessionParamTranslator.java:84) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearch.translatePathValueKeys(FlexibleSearch.java:2542) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearch.executeSearchUsingFlexibleSearchDialect(FlexibleSearch.java:1705) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearch.executeSearchUsingPolyglotDialect(FlexibleSearch.java:1540) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearch.search(FlexibleSearch.java:1471) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearch.search(FlexibleSearch.java:1459) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.flexiblesearch.FlexibleSearch.search(FlexibleSearch.java:1431) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.util.OneToManyHandler.getSearchResult(OneToManyHandler.java:302) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.util.OneToManyHandler.getValues(OneToManyHandler.java:283) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.catalog.jalo.GeneratedCatalog.getCatalogVersions(GeneratedCatalog.java:190) ~[catalogserver.jar:?]
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) ~[?:?]
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:77) ~[?:?]
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43) ~[?:?]
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568) ~[?:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.type.ReflectionAttributeAccess$AttributeMethod.invokeGetter(ReflectionAttributeAccess.java:753) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.type.ReflectionAttributeAccess.getValue(ReflectionAttributeAccess.java:959) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.jalo.Item.getAttribute(Item.java:1922) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.internal.model.impl.JaloPersistenceObject.readRawValue(JaloPersistenceObject.java:110) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.internal.converter.impl.ItemModelConverter.readSingleAttribute(ItemModelConverter.java:1456) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.internal.converter.impl.ItemAttributeProvider.getAttribute(ItemAttributeProvider.java:110) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.model.ItemModelContextImpl.loadUnlocalizedAttribute(ItemModelContextImpl.java:285) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.model.ItemModelContextImpl.getValue(ItemModelContextImpl.java:245) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.model.ItemModelContextImpl.getPropertyValue(ItemModelContextImpl.java:261) ~[coreserver.jar:?]
	at de.hybris.platform.catalog.model.CatalogModel.getCatalogVersions(CatalogModel.java:217) ~[models.jar:?]

 I am using Commerce version2211.35.

entry.title: Deep entity search in Get Record Collection in SAP BUILD Apps
entry.content:
Hello fellow Builders,

I have a seemingly common looking scenario, yet i couldn't find much pointers on the community on this issue. So thought of putting it here to get help. 

I have a build app scenario, where i have a Goods Recipient as a only input available and based on that I have to call MaterialDocumentHeader Api. I am going to pass some other inputs to this API as a FILTER, 4 of them are from the deep entity MaterialDocumentItem of this API and one of them is from the header part. 

I tried to use 2 ways to call this API in Build apps:

1) With S4HANA destination configured in BTP and calling it directly with the below option.

In this case, i see that there are options such as Query parameters and Filter condition, but not sure how to access the deep structure in this case. I have already enabled the Expand item entity option as below:

 

2) With Create entity -> SAP BTP Destination REST API integration option 

In this case, i am using query.search.query option as rightly suggested by @Dan_Wroblewski , and then gave the SEARCH url with options such as expand and filter like below:

but the moment i access the deep entity attribute such as plant like this, the odata call fails. Any pointers on how these calls should be made with Build apps formulas? Your inputs are highly appreciated. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: How to bind RAP v4 oData action with custom dialog us...
entry.content:
It helped.
Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP IntegrationSolutions Addon Open Allocation Button
entry.content: May I know where did you download the IntegrationSolutionsAddOn.Ard?

entry.title: Xstep. How to get context of XSTEP Tree via ABAP tools like XS_GET_CONTEXT or any others...
entry.content:
Dear all

i'm struggling to get to the way how to interpret data from XS_GET_CONTEXT which is returning context object in a complex multireference embedded structure....

I need to have access to Control_recipe context releven sections of XSTEP tree to have some custom checks during Control Recipe generation (CONTROL_RECIPE_CREATE FM which is triggered in Process order release).

Using XS_GET_CONTEXT of CL_COCR_CMX_TOP class gives IO_CONTEXT object with copmlex embedded structure with nodes: OBJECT, CLC_CMX_CX_NODE, CLC_CMX_CX_NODE_LEAF, CLC_CMX_CX_CONTEXT

and i managed to find steps of level 1 (where root step i treat as level 0) when i click on M_PARENT under CLC_CMX_CX_NODE and in M_PARENT under LCL_CMX_PERSISTENT node there is M_PERSISTENT_CHILDS table with lines refering to STEPS of Xtree level 1.

If i use XS_GET_CONTEXT_FROM_NODE  method of the same class, then it seems that using CM_SESSION under M_PARENT gives M_PERSISTENT object with reference to all steps in XSTEP tree, but only to the header of step and not to the end level of details (refer to CM_Session_all_steps.png).

Could you please help to understand the following:

1) how to read entire Xstep tree data with abap tools from Process order

2) how to read specific Xstep step from header level to the final level of details (process instructions assigned, etc, etc).

3) how to interpet objects like IO_CONTEXT, IO_NODE described above... 

Thanks a lot in advance,

KR, Andrew.

entry.title: Re: ATP & MRP Areas
entry.content:
Hi Nansy_Matary,

from the error message it seems that somehow SBC is being active. Its either a bug (in that case we need to investigate the issue via SAP support case - please create a new case if required) or perhaps one of your substituted products has SBC active. Verify the possible substitution material/plant combinations and verify the assigned checking groups for SBC activation.

best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Fiori-Style Analytics Dashboard: 'Invalid Username/Pa...
entry.content:
Hi Sisira,

Yes, PAL_ROLE and AFL execution rights required for non-manager users as well.

GRANT PAL_ROLE TO "<USER>";
GRANT AFL__SYS_AFL_AFLPAL_EXECUTE TO "<USER>";

Non-manager users may not have the required database privileges, while the Manager user does.

Thanks,

Jakes

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements: returning values when doing intent ba...
entry.content: This is how we did it in our evaluations and unfortunately this is still a forward navigation. Hitting the back button will bring back the user to app B, then A, then homescreen with all steps which he made inbetween. Desired result would be that being back in A and hitting the back button brings the user to the home screen or wherever he came from.

entry.title: Re: Blog it Forward - Raquel Cunha - version 2025
entry.content: Quite motivating.. Thank you @Raquel_Pereira_da_Cunha

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Demo Guides
entry.content:
Hi Greg_not_so,

the appliance is the on-premise release of S/4, hence it‘s not a hosted offering.
CAL lets you create a pre-configured image very quickly but the hardware for it (ie. the hyperscaler) needs to come from you. 
Re. a „free“ system, you may try the S/4 public cloud trial to see if this fits your needs: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana/trial.html

Best, Joerg

entry.title: Re: list UIBB multiple values selection and data transfer to another list UIBB
entry.content:
Hi Chandra,

I need your help as you have mentioned using single selection you have transferred from list uibb to form uibb

It is the case I was working now it will be helpful that if you can help me out to achieve

Thanks & Regards 
Dhanush

entry.title: No show status in employee profile can the candidate be moved out from hired status
entry.content:
Hi,

if a new hire status is changed as "No Show" in employee profile, is it feasible to move that candidate from hired status in Job Requisition to decline status when job requisition is closed.

Any thoughts to achieve the above. SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding  SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting 

thank you

Regards

Vish

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Singapore Peppol eInvoices (InvoiceNow) - Part 1
entry.content:
@PranavSanyo 

In the SAP DRC Peppol Dashboard, choose the test environment and you can register with prefix "SGTST"+UEN e.g. SGTST202566666D (Not SGUEN+UEN) and choose option "Sign with PDF". 

You need to upload letter of authorization in PDF form, see sample of authorization letter at Link 

This is for testing purpose. For actual registration, corppass authentication is required.

entry.title: Re: No Shows after 30 days of Hire date

From what I have read, it seems like IntegrationSolutionsAddon.ard is one of the add-on to be installed if I want to use the integration solution, but I am not able to find the installer in the sap portal or the standard SAP installer.

if there is anyone who knows where I can get the IntegrationSolutionsAddon.ard, can you be kind enough to give me steps to reach the installer in the SAP portal?

entry.title: Re: Eclipse BW Bridge Project: Logon to the service insta...
entry.content: mitko1994, have you found a solution for this? I'm facing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Understanding OAuth 2.0 Behavior in SAP Datasphere’s Generic OData Connection
entry.content:
Hello @marcw15 ,

in your opinion, is it better to extract data from OData with a dataflow (and add some parameters to make more reliable the connection)

or with a remote table through Odata Adapter?

Which is the most solid option?

We find that many times we get less rows than expected or randomly failures. sometimes we need to add as parameter $inlinecount = allpages or $count , other times we add batch query options.

I agree with you that it lacks transparency, sometimes we need to capture the calls and try them with postman

entry.title: ObjectTable swipe behaviour not working
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing an issue with implementing swipe behaviour in ObjectTable.

Scenario:

  1. I have added a ObjectCell to display list of notifications.
  2. If the user right swipes on a row, user to able to delete the notification.
  3. After clicking on a single row of list, user is navigated to detail screen.
  4. Once user comes back from detail screen, the above clicked row item is not showing swipe behaviour. If the user tries to swipes on other rows, the behaviour is working as expected.

Please provide your guidance.

MDK SDK used: 24.11.2-002

entry.title: Re: Eclipse BW Bridge Project: Logon to the service insta...
entry.content: @SylviaD, For me it was an authorizations problem which was resolved.

entry.title: HTML company code logo image in the invoice form for interactive signs through Digital Signature
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

We have implemented the Digital Signature at company code level, however the client has one root certificate across all 5 company codes. Client requirement is to showcase 5 different company code logo images in the invoice form and link it with Digital signature .pfx file. We informed them to have 5 diff root certificate for the same. Also we cannot display any image and hyperlink the digital signature on it.

Experts please suggest how to achieve this or any workaround we can do. I am unable to get any appropriate SAP blog to convince our client.

entry.title: Re: Universal Display Hierarchy - Display values on all h...
entry.content:
Hi,

The described behavior occurs, if the Totals are hidden. You need to enable the display of Totals in the properties of the child (!) dimension to see numbers at parent level.

regards

entry.title: Re: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content: I suggest to debug inbound IDOC posting and check where this value for vendor number comes from. Unfortunately without access to the system, I can only guess what is wrong.

entry.title: Re: Why SAP BDC for Consuming SAP Data via CDS Views and not Replicate in Non-SAP Platform
entry.content:
some ponts are not true, 

before RFC/ODP limitation note to third party systems, the fields from CDS Views have business names => the fact that the fields "have SAP technical name" is not true. 

The real problem is that, the choice of the data warehouse is secondary, the primary choice is the front end tool for BI!!

If a customer wants to stay with a vendor that is different from SAP in the front end, it will crush against SAP limitation on usage of ODP, it will require to install Datasphere and pay "premium outbound flows" just to export HIS (customer) data to another data warehouse. the other option is to use Odata but it requires many manual activities in SAP S/4 to expose CDS views as ODP with delta in Odata (& it requires to manage complex recovery flows in the third party ETL / DWH tool).

This cost of "premium outbound flows" or manual activities to bypass RFC/ODP limitation can be soo high that customer may want to keep using SAP Data Services to extract data through ODP instead of "buying" Datasphere.

entry.title: Data Access Control in Data Builder and Authorization Scenario in Business Builder - SAP Datasphere.
entry.content:
Hello All,

Welcome back to my other blog post on an exciting feature available in SAP Datasphere: the data access control in Data Builder and the authorization scenario in Business Builder. This blog post will explain this concept with an example and demo.

In this blog post, I am not going to discuss what data access control in data builder is and what the authorization scenario in business builder is. You can go to the official documentation of SAP and can understand the concept. 

We will develop a business case in this blog post, which can also assist you in developing your scenario for a real-time project. Now, straight away, let us get into the demo and understand in detail.

First, let us create a product category hierarchy table, as in this post I will explain DAC based on hierarchy, and then we will move into creating a DAC based on a table and then an authorization scenario in Business Builder.

Now let us go to the dataset of this table.

So my hierarchy table is created, and it is ready.

To further implement DAC, we need a control table that will control the access; in short, we are going to create a permission table. This is a must when we create DAC, as it is going to control the activity and will provide row-level access to users who actually want to view the data. you can read more about permission table and its usage.

Now let us create a permission table.

Business Name : Permission Entity Product Category

Technical Name: PE_ProductCategory

 So now our control table or permission table is created.

Now we are set to create our data access control object, which we are going to apply to a final view. So let us create a data access control. Go to data builder and create a new DAC.

So we are all set with our data access control now. Now let us create a view and apply the DAC to the view.

So, I have created a view with the name Sales View Fact, and I have a product table. Kindly see below.

 

we are going to join this 2 tables with Product ID

 

Now let us apply the data access control to the final view. Please follow the image below.

Now let us view the data in the Sales View Fact table.

 

So, we have successfully implemented the data access control based on a parent-child hierarchy. Similarly, we can maintain many user ids as per requirement and can view the data applying data access control to users.

Now, in the next blogpost, I shall create again a data access control and apply the data access control to a new authorization scenario in Business Builder.

Thank You.

Narasingha.

entry.title: Re: Error message requesting more days than you have in y...
entry.content:
Hi @camilaromero yes, we find a workaround. Maybe there are more ways but ew have solve with this one.

We have created a take tule inside the time type in scope, that check the balance and then you can put the translation in Norwegian with the message that you want. And to avoid double message (because the one by default in the system will be shown) you can put the cannot fall below with a certain number in the time account type, to make sure that it is not going to affect the first check. 

If you have different time types linked to time account types that should be review, make sure to put in different rules because if not the rule is going to take the full balance of all the time types inside.

The take rule is a simple one, with a "calculate balance for types" , with a create date from the beginning of the year till end of year to take the full time account balance, and with <= 0. then you can select here the time account in scope. And "or" condition linked to the previous one, that check the "employee time.number of days" > calculate balance for types (and the same create date, from the beginning till end of year). Then, Raise a message with the custom message that you will put. 

The only problem with this is that you need to take into account the cannot fall below number and make sure that this is not going to impact your configuration. In our case, will not affect because it is not a real cannot fall below, the check it is going to be done in the take rule with the balance. 

 

I hope this helps you 🙂 let me know if you try this solution finally

 

BR,

Nuria

entry.title: Re: MDG-M Standard Fields are not editable
entry.content:
Hi Rei,

the field properties BAdI in the method IF_EX_USMD_ACC_FLD_PROP_CDS~MODIFY_FLD_PROP_ATTR does not belong to the FPM. Hence, I cannot answer your question why it only works for the Basic Data.

Best regards
Jens

entry.title: Re: Eclipse BW Bridge Project: Logon to the service insta...
entry.content: @mitko1994, thanks for the answer. What kind of authorization problem wa it? Was it an issue with the user for the BW-Bridge Cockpit or something else?

entry.title: Credit Check for hierarchical group of customer!
entry.content:
Use Case:

Credit check for a group of customers: many times, an organization wants to do credit check at a group level, when the customer is group of organization having multiple sub-units. This scenario arises when the customer is a holding company having more than one subsidiary and the seller company wants to maintain a floating credit limit for the subsidiaries and an absolute limit at the holding company level.

Example:

In the table at the group level the credit limit is USD 160, and this is total of the credit limits at the subsidiary level. The idea is when one of the subsidiaries run out of credit the credit analyst can manually adjust the limit to the extent of available limits with the other subsidiaries and yet the total limit at the group level remains at USD 160. Credit limits for a BP can be maintained in the app ‘Manage Credit Accounts’. 

Master Data Setup and Configuration:

The group structure can be maintained by defining the BP relationships: maximum 10 levels of hierarchy can be maintained. This hierarchy can also be imported via an excel sheet. The data must be maintained in the template provided in the app ‘Import Credit Account Hierarchies’. The relationship categories being used here are ‘Superordinate Credit Account From’ and ‘Subordinate Credit Account To’.

Once we maintain this relationship for the Superordinate Credit Account From, the relationship for the Subordinate Credit Account To is automatically maintained in the system, as shown in the snapshot:

The credit account hierarchy can also be seen in the app ‘manage credit accounts’ under a credit segment:

Test the scenario:

Our objective is to block a sales order (SO) not only at the sub-unit level but also at the group level in case the credit limit fails. Usually, the group level credit limit is higher than the individual units and the credit analyst keeps a floating limit for the sub-units to maximize the sales.

When we create an SO for a subunit the credit limit is not only checked at the sub0unit level but also at the group level.

Case: When the credit check fails at the sub-unit level but not at the group level. In this case the credit analyst can manually adjust the credit limit of the entire group keeping the group level limit same and yet approve the SO at the sub-unit level:

Case: When credit check fails for sub-unit as well as at the group level: in such cases the SO cannot go through and needs other measures to release the case.

Note: the messages in the above test cases appear after changing the total SO value (to demonstrate both the cases).

Conclusion:

It is possible to perform a credit check at a group level for complex group structure kind of customers. However manual adjustments for maintaining the floating credit limits are required, nonetheless it serves the purpose!

entry.title: Filter query not working after setting target specifier in List Picker
entry.content:
Hi,

Scenario:

We are having two listpicker components: 1. for state and 2. for location. We want to populate location list once user selects state from state listpicker. To achieve this we have followed this blog.
And this is working as expected.

We also have implemented rule to make location listpicker's search functionality case insensitive. But once user selects state, this rule defined for search is not working as expected as it gets overridden on the value change event of state list picker.

Please guide us to resolve this issue.

MDK version: 24.11.2-002

Regards,

Girish

entry.title: Re: MISSING MANDATE REFERENCE
entry.content:
Hello Davide,

Thanks for the question. 

After talking to the experts, I can provide the following answer: The SEPA Mandate related functionality is not implemented in the "Upload Customer Open Items" and "Create Outgoing Invoices" (FB70) apps.

You may request features on the Customer Influence Portal. We continuously review and prioritize feature requests based on customer feedback.

Kind regards,

Anne

entry.title: Re: CJ20N: Zugriffskontrollliste (ACL)
entry.content:
Hallo, 

die ACL ist meines Wissens veraltet. Hier findest du weitere Infos: 

522426 - Beratung: Berechtigungen im Projektsystem - SAP for Me

Zudem gibt es eine Erweiterung, welche die über den Tcode CMOD ausprägen kannst: 

Die Erweiterung CNEX0002 enthält folgende Komponenten:

 o   EXIT_SAPLCNAU_001: PS Customer-Exit zur Berechtigungsprüfung der Projektdefinition

 o   EXIT_SAPLCNAU_002: PS Customer-Exit zur Berechtigungsprüfung von PSP-Elementen

 o   EXIT_SAPLCNAU_003: PS Customer-Exit zur Berechtigungsprüfung des Netzplankopfs

 o   EXIT_SAPLCNAU_004: PS Customer-Exit zur Berechtigungsprüfung von Vorgänge im Netzplan

 o   EXIT_SAPLCNAU_005: PS Customer-Exit zur Berechtigungsprüfung von Meilensteinen

 o   EXIT_SAPLCNAU_006: PS Customer-Exit zur Berechtigungsprüfung von PS-Texten

 Diesen Funktionsbausteinen wird neben den Attributen des zu prüfenden

 Objekts (z.B. PSP-Element oder Vorgang) weitere Information übergeben:

 o   Aktivität

     Die Berechtigungsaktivität unterscheidet, wie auf die Information

     zugegriffen wird (anzeigen, ändern). Bitte entnehmen sie die

     verwendeten Werte der Dokumentation der

     Standard-Berechtigungsobjekte des Projekt Systems.

 o   Messagetyp

     Dieser Parameter bestimmt den Messagetyp mit dem eine Meldung (z.B

     bei fehlender Berechtigung) auszugeben ist.

 o   Rückgabewerte

     Der Funktionsbaustein muß ein Kennzeichen (X) zurückliefern, das

     gesetzt ist, wenn der Benutzer die entsprechende Berechtigung hat.

Hinterlasse gerne Kudos, wenn dir die Infos weitergeholfen haben. 

Viele Grüße 

Tobias

entry.title: New Region cn20 Now Available on SAP BTP in China, Powered by Microsoft Azure!
entry.content:
News

We are excited to announce the availability of a new region, technically named cn20, on the BTP (Business Technology Platform) in China. This new region, built on Microsoft Azure and referred to as China North 3 (Hebei), is now open for BTP customers. This expansion offers BTP customers more options and greater flexibility.

Please find the available regions on the BTP in China below:

IaaS ProviderRegionRegion NameTechnical KeyTechnical Key of IaaS ProviderNAT IPs (egress, IPs for requests from a Cloud Foundry app)LB IPs (ingress, for incoming requests)API EndpointDomainCockpit Logon
Microsoft Azurecn20China North 3 (Hebei)cf-cn20ChinaNorth3159.27.65.75, 159.27.107.117, 159.27.217.233143.64.224.195api.cf.cn20.platform.sapcloud.cncn20.platform.sapcloud.cnOpen Cockpit
Alibaba Cloudcn40China (Shanghai)cf-cn40cn-shanghai101.132.190.155, 106.14.165.33, 106.14.184.113139.224.7.71api.cf.cn40.platform.sapcloud.cncn40.platform.sapcloud.cnOpen Cockpit

 

How to Consume Region CN20 on BTP in China

You can deploy applications in different regions. Each region represents a geographical location (for example, Europe, US East) where applications, data, or services are hosted. Regions are provided either by SAP or by our Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) partners Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Alibaba Cloud. The third-party region providers operate the infrastructure layer of the regions, whereas SAP operates the platform layer and Cloud Foundry.

A region is chosen at the subaccount level. For each subaccount, you select exactly one region. The selection of a region is dependent on many factors: For example, application performance (response time, latency) can be optimized by selecting a region close to the user.

Note that the BTP in China operates independently from other regions. This means you can only select and use regions within China (specifically cn20 or cn40) under a Global Account designated for China. In other words, you cannot use a Global Account created for other regions to consume cn20 or cn40 in China. You can identify the Global Account by the Cockpit link. For China, the link is https://cockpit.cn40.platform.sapcloud.cn/cockpit. Note that both cn20 and cn40 regions share the same Cockpit link with the subdomain “cn40,” as cn40 is considered the central region, while cn20 is the local region. These distinctions are mainly for internal account management purposes and do not require much attention from your side.

Step 1: Open the Global Account on the BTP in China

Open the Cockpit via the entry link: https://cockpit.cn40.platform.sapcloud.cn/cockpit.

For more information, see Managing Global Accounts and Subaccounts Using the Cockpit.

Step 2: Create a Subaccount and Select Region

On your Global Account page, click on the Create → Subaccount button.

Provide the required details for your new Subaccount, and choose the region that you need.

For example: cn20-live

For more information, see Managing Global Accounts and Subaccounts Using the Cockpit.

Step 3: Enable Cloud Foundry

Click on the tile of your subaccount, and then click on Enable Cloud Foundry.

Enter a name for the organization.

Click Create.

Then you will find that the API Endpoint is https://api.cf.cn20.platform.sapcloud.cn, which is hosted on Microsoft Azure.

Next, you can continue with your consumption of the BTP.

 

FAQ

Q: If I want to use cn20, can I reuse the Global Account outside of the BTP in China?

A: No. The BTP in China operates independently from other regions. This means you can only select and use regions within China (specifically cn20 or cn40) under a Global Account designated for China. In other words, you cannot use a Global Account created for other regions to consume cn20 or cn40 in China. You can identify the Global Account by the Cockpit link. For China, the link is https://cockpit.cn40.platform.sapcloud.cn/cockpit. Note that both cn20 and cn40 regions share the same Cockpit link with the subdomain “cn40,” as cn40 is considered the central region, while cn20 is the local region. These distinctions are mainly for internal account management purposes and do not require much attention from your side.

Q: Do I need a new Global Account to use cn20?

A: No. If you have already been using cn40, it means that you already have a Global Account on the BTP in China. You simply need to create a subaccount for cn20 under this Global Account.

Q: I have configured a custom domain using SAP Custom Domain Service on cn40 to deploy applications. Do I need to recreate the custom domain and complete the filing procedures on cn20?

A: Yes. You still need to follow the steps in this blog to configure and file the domain: SAP BTP上实现自定义域名配置. The difference is that you need to complete the filing procedures on Azure.

Q: Can the SKUs purchased for cn40 also be used on cn20?

A: The consumption remains the same, but the billing is done separately for each region. For example, if you want to use the Integration Suite in both cn40 and cn20, you need to purchase two quotas of the Integration Suite.

Q: Are CPEA or BTPEA available on cn20?

A: Similar to cn40, currently, only the subscription commercial model is supported in China.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Integration with SAP Apps and 3rd Party Apps !
entry.content:
The main concern right now is the very limited availability of source systems for pushing non-SAP data into the Datasphere object store(HDLF). This is crucial because the object store enables zero-copy/delta sharing while leveraging the medallion architecture on cost-effective HDLF storage.

Additionally, sharing Datasphere HANA database tables with the object store is not possible, making it challenging to integrate other source data using Data Flow. This limitation arises because Data Flow is not applicable for object store spaces, restricting its usage to HANA disk-based Datasphere spaces. Given the need to support a large number of source systems, it would be more practical to enable Data Flow and object data sharing from non-HDLF to HDLF spaces.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse BW Bridge Project: Logon to the service insta...
entry.content: I don't know to be honest. My colleagues who deal with authorizations resolved it for me. Perhaps you can trace it in a log or something?

entry.title: S-User permission to access Info Center (Enable now)
entry.content: Which permissions need to be granted to the S-User to have access to the Enable Now Info Center?

entry.title: Re: Empty profit center from billing documents gets overw...
entry.content: To add to it, it is a good practice to use a dummy profit centre rather than leaving it blank.
